marketState: neutral-to-bearish — hot near-term weather offsets a bearish storage/technical backdrop
summary: Front-month NG trades almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.899 vs $2.917 fair, band $2.417-$3.417) after a -14.9% YTD decline versus a -6.9% seasonal median, and seasonality reliability is low this week (59pp 10-90% spread) so the seasonal edge is weak. Technicals are uniformly bearish across all six timeframes (bearish trend, EMA50<200, mostly negative MACD) even though 1-7 day cooling demand is running +23.4% above the 5-year average near the top of its range — a near-term bullish weather tailwind that fades to roughly normal by days 8-16 (-3.3% vs 5y). Storage is 184 Bcf above the 5-year average and just posted a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, reinforcing a soft medium-term supply backdrop, while managed-money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of the past 5 years, leaving room to add shorts rather than being crowded. The futures curve is roughly flat-to-slightly-backwardated through fall before a large contango builds into winter (Jan27 $4.175), a normal seasonal shape rather than a distress signal. Net: bearish storage/technical/seasonality-tilt data outweighs the transient near-term weather boost, favoring a cautious short bias for the multi-week horizon and a wait for confirmation on the near-term trade given the weather-vs-technicals conflict.

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.95 (sell on a bounce toward fair-value midpoint)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; scale out ~50% at TP1
longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y) with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise, unanimous bearish trend across all 6 technical timeframes (EMA50<200, mostly negative MACD), and a modest bearish seasonality tilt (38% odds of higher price in 4wks, typical 4w move -1.4%) outweigh a fair-value reading showing price already roughly FAIR rather than cheap. Managed-money is net short but only at the 35th 5y percentile, leaving room for further short positioning without extreme-crowding risk.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close back above ~$3.15 (reclaiming the EMA50/200 zone) or a re-acceleration in 8-16D weather demand (back above +10% vs 5y) would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7 day cooling demand is +23.4% vs the 5-year average near the top of its range (rangePos 0.89) and the front contract sits at a slight backwardation to next month, both consistent with near-term weather support; but all six technical timeframes are bearish and price already sits at fair value, leaving no clean directional edge.
shortTerm.invalidation: n/a while in WAIT; reassess if weather forecasts shift materially or technicals realign.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) intraday RSI (1h/4h) to reclaim 50 with MACD histogram turning positive, confirming the heat-driven demand bounce, or (b) a confirmed break below ~$2.85 with continued bearish MACD to align technicals with a short entry.

keyDrivers: 1-7D cooling demand +23.4% vs 5y (rangePos 0.89) — near-term bullish weather tailwind
Storage +184 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build — medium-term bearish supply overhang
All 6 technical timeframes bearish (EMA50<200, mostly negative MACD) — broad-based bearish momentum
Front price essentially at fair value ($2.899 vs $2.917 fair) — no valuation edge either direction
Managed-money net short only at 35th 5y percentile — positioning not crowded, room to extend
risks: Low seasonality reliability this week (59pp 10-90% spread) — seasonal signal is noisy
8-16D weather demand already easing back to near-normal (-3.3% vs 5y); a hot-spell extension could flip near-term bias bullish
Large winter contango (Jan27 $4.175) means an early cold-snap repricing could spike front-month volatility against a short
